segments.text: And Job continued his discourse:
As surely as God lives, who has denied me justice, the Almighty, who has made my life bitter,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: as long as I have life within me, the breath of God in my nostrils,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: my lips will not say anything wicked, and my tongue will not utter lies.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I will never admit you are in the right; till I die, I will not deny my integrity.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I will maintain my innocence and never let go of it; my conscience will not reproach me as long as I live.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: May my enemy be like the wicked, my adversary like the unjust!
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: For what hope have the godless when they are cut off, when God takes away their life?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Does God listen to their cry when distress comes upon them?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Will they find delight in the Almighty? Will they call on God at all times?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I will teach you about the power of God; the ways of the Almighty I will not conceal.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: You have all seen this yourselves. Why then this meaningless talk?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Here is the fate God allots to the wicked, the heritage a ruthless man receives from the Almighty:
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: However many his children, their fate is the sword; his offspring will never have enough to eat.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: The plague will bury those who survive him, and their widows will not weep for them.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Though he heaps up silver like dust and clothes like piles of clay,
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: what he lays up the righteous will wear, and the innocent will divide his silver.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: The house he builds is like a moth's cocoon, like a hut made by a watchman.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: He lies down wealthy, but will do so no more; when he opens his eyes, all is gone.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Terrors overtake him like a flood; a tempest snatches him away in the night.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: The east wind carries him off, and he is gone; it sweeps him out of his place.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: It hurls itself against him without mercy as he flees headlong from its power.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: It claps its hands in derision and hisses him out of his place.
segments.speaker: Job
